In my opinion this university is currently not equipped to host international students. There are many instances of inadvertent discrimination and exclusion (eg. being refused entry to student societies due to not speaking Chinese, many training sessions and talks are in Chinese only resulting in loss of opportunities, unable to be on the student union commitee, missing out on voting for the best teacher as the link was only shared in Chinese etc., students struggling to get internships or lab experience due to noone feeling comfortable supervising in English) I feel that my ability to learn is severely impaired. It is hard to join in discussions in the lab or have discussions about my work with colleagues or collaborators. I know more about my friends work abroad than I know about the work of my fellow lab members. This is not due to malice, my lab mates are all very kind, but the language barrier is huge and makes it difficult. The university is also not doing enough to support international students. There is inadequate support prior to joining (eg. no checklists are provided about what to prepare and what apps to get) and after joining. It would be good to create a guide for international students explaining to them about life here eg. about drinking water, how to get food, pay, information about the medical system and about precautions to take (eg. rabies is endemic etc). International students are extremely vulnerable and the university is currently not helping enough. Even at the HKUST (GZ) clinic it can be complicated if you do not speak Chinese. I know that none of this is a deliberate attempt to exclude but at times it can be extremely frustrating and I can't help but feel that some of these points should have been thought through and have been addressed. I have considered leaving because of all this but I like my research project and my supervisor and I want to finish my degree.
